vBRIEFInfo.description: Deft Directive Phases 1-3 specification. Converted from legacy flat format to vBRIEF v0.5.

narratives.Overview: Deft Directive is a Markdown framework for AI agents to use when generating software. It defines layered behavioral rules, workflow strategies, and quality gates across four components: (1) the Markdown framework (primary product — .md files consumed by agents at runtime), (2) the Python CLI (`run` — terminal setup and spec generation), (3) the Go installer (`cmd/deft-install/` — standalone binary for end-user install), and (4) the test suite (`tests/` — CLI and content validation). This specification covers Phase 1 (bug fixes and adoption blockers), Phase 2 (content fixes), and Phase 3 (CI). Phases 4–5 are deferred; see PRD.md and #67 for scope boundaries. References: PRD.md, .planning/codebase/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/research/deft-directive-research.md.
narratives.Architecture: Four components. (1) Markdown framework: .md files organized by domain (languages/, strategies/, templates/, skills/, context/, meta/, vbrief/). Rule precedence: USER.md Personal > PROJECT.md > USER.md Defaults > language.md > tool.md > main.md > specification.md. (2) Python CLI (run): cmd_* functions, ask_input/ask_choice/ask_confirm for interaction, _atomic_write for file output, _resume_or_ask for resume support. Extension-less; test shim via run.py importlib. (3) Go installer (cmd/deft-install/): Wizard → CloneDeft/UpdateDeft → WriteAgentsMD → WriteAgentsSkills → CreateUserConfigDir. Stdlib-only. Platform files: drives_windows.go / drives_other.go. (4) Tests: CLI tests (tests/cli/) use isolated_env + mock_user_input fixtures; content tests (tests/content/) validate .md structure, links, schema compliance.

items.title: Add inference boundary guards to deft-setup SKILL.md Phase 2 (FR-1, FR-2)

narrative.Description: Add ⊗ rules to the Inference section of Phase 2 in skills/deft-setup/SKILL.md: never scan ./deft/ for build files; never run git commands inside ./deft/. Only inspect project root and non-deft subdirectories. Closes #79.
narrative.Acceptance: SKILL.md Phase 2 Inference section contains ⊗ rule: MUST NOT scan ./deft/ for go.mod, package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, *.csproj; SKILL.md Phase 2 Inference section contains ⊗ rule: MUST NOT run git commands inside ./deft/; tests/content/test_skills.py passes

items.title: Add project name fallback prompt when no build files detected (FR-3)

narrative.Description: Update deft-setup SKILL.md Phase 2 to prompt the user for a project name when codebase inference finds no build files at the project root. Currently falls through to deft internals. Closes #80.
narrative.Acceptance: Phase 2 Question Sequence includes explicit fallback: if no build files found at project root, ask user to provide project name; Fallback prompt appears in Track 1, Track 2, and Track 3 paths; tests/cli/test_project.py covers no-build-files scenario

items.title: Remove Primary Languages from USER.md template and Phase 1 interview (FR-4)

narrative.Description: Language is a project-level concern determined per-project via codebase inference, not a user preference. Remove from USER.md template, Phase 1 Track 1 Step 2, Track 2 Step 2. Update Phase 2 Step 3 to always infer first. Closes #107.
narrative.Acceptance: USER.md template in SKILL.md no longer includes **Primary Languages** field; Phase 1 Track 1 has no Step 2 asking about languages; Phase 1 Track 2 has no language step; Phase 2 Step 3 infers from codebase; falls back to open ask (no USER.md default pre-fill); tests/cli/test_bootstrap.py updated; no language question in happy path

items.title: Add deployment platform question before language in deft-setup Phase 2 (FR-5)

narrative.Description: Ask deployment platform (web, mobile, desktop, embedded, CLI, cloud service, other) before asking about language. Platform context narrows language shortlist. Track 1 only. Closes #108.
narrative.Acceptance: Phase 2 Track 1 Question Sequence: deployment platform question precedes language question; Platform answer informs language shortlist shown in next question; Track 2 and Track 3 unaffected (simplified paths)

items.title: Audit and fix vBRIEF generation in cmd_spec (FR-6)

narrative.Description: The run script's cmd_spec generates specification.vbrief.json. Audit the output format against spec_validate.py and vbrief/vbrief.md. Ensure: vBRIEFInfo envelope with version 0.5; plan object with title, status, items; task status values from valid enum (pending/running/completed/blocked/cancelled). The legacy 'todo'/'doing'/'done' values from old vBRIEF must not be used. Closes #72 (CLI path).
narrative.Acceptance: task spec:validate passes on all cmd_spec output; task spec:render succeeds on approved spec; tests/cli/test_spec.py covers vBRIEF output format

items.title: Audit and fix vBRIEF generation in deft-setup Phase 3 (FR-6)

narrative.Description: The deft-setup skill Phase 3 also generates specification.vbrief.json. Same audit as t1.2.1 for the agent-skill path. Update skills/deft-setup/SKILL.md Output sections to reference the correct schema. Closes #72 (agent skill path).
narrative.Acceptance: SKILL.md Phase 3 Output sections reference correct vBRIEF field names (vBRIEFInfo envelope with plan object containing title, status, items; pending/running/completed/blocked/cancelled for task status, not legacy todo/doing/done); tests/content/test_vbrief_schema.py assertions strengthened to catch field name violations

items.title: Fix run bootstrap infinite loop when strategies/ is empty (FR-7)

narrative.Description: cmd_bootstrap enters an infinite loop when get_available_strategies() returns an empty list or the strategies/ directory is unresolvable. Add a guard: if no strategies found, default to 'interview' and warn rather than looping. Closes #91, #92.
narrative.Acceptance: cmd_bootstrap completes without looping when strategies/ is empty; Fallback to 'interview' strategy is logged as a warning; tests/cli/test_bootstrap.py covers empty-strategies-dir scenario

items.title: Add version display to all run CLI commands on startup (FR-10)

narrative.Description: All cmd_* functions should print the VERSION on entry (e.g. 'Deft CLI v0.4.2'). Note: VERSION in run is currently 0.4.2; this value is provisional and will display behind the framework's 0.5.2 until version unification is addressed (see PRD Open Question 1). Closes #49.
narrative.Acceptance: All cmd_* functions print version string on startup; Version string format: 'Deft CLI v{VERSION}'; tests/cli/test_import_smoke.py or per-command tests assert version output

items.title: Merge strategies/default.md into strategies/interview.md and remove default.md (FR-8)

narrative.Description: default.md is a duplicate of interview.md. Merge any unique content into interview.md, then delete default.md. Update any references to default.md. Closes #31.
narrative.Acceptance: strategies/default.md no longer exists; strategies/interview.md contains all content from former default.md (or supersedes it); No .md file in the repo references strategies/default.md; tests/content/test_structure.py updated to not assert default.md exists; test passes

items.title: Update strategies/brownfield.md to redirect to strategies/map.md (FR-9)

narrative.Description: brownfield.md is a legacy alias for map.md. Replace content with a short redirect note pointing to map.md. Do not delete (backward compatibility for any existing references). Closes #50 (brownfield portion).
narrative.Acceptance: strategies/brownfield.md contains only a redirect to strategies/map.md; strategies/map.md is the canonical document; tests/content/test_standards.py passes

items.title: Write coding/build-output.md — build output validation directive (FR-11)

narrative.Description: New file documenting rules for validating build output (dist/, bin/, artifacts). Agents must: verify expected artifacts exist post-build, check artifact sizes are non-zero, fail loudly on silent build failures. Closes #105.
narrative.Acceptance: coding/build-output.md exists with RFC2119 legend; Contains ! rules for: verifying artifact existence, checking non-zero size, failing on missing expected outputs; Referenced from coding/coding.md

items.title: Document toolchain validation gate in framework (FR-12)

narrative.Description: Create a new coding/toolchain.md requiring agents to verify required tools are installed before beginning implementation. Reference it from coding/coding.md. Closes #106.
narrative.Acceptance: Framework contains ! rule: before implementation begins, verify all required tools are available (e.g. go version, uv --version, task --version); Rule lives in a new coding/toolchain.md, referenced (linked) from coding/coding.md

items.title: Update all stale core/user.md and core/project.md references to canonical paths (FR-13)

narrative.Description: Find all .md references to core/user.md and core/project.md legacy paths. Replace with canonical paths: ~/.config/deft/USER.md (or %APPDATA%\deft\USER.md on Windows) and ./PROJECT.md respectively. Closes #58.
narrative.Acceptance: grep for 'core/user.md' returns zero matches in non-history .md files (except legacy fallback note in SKILL.md); grep for 'core/project.md' returns zero matches in non-history .md files (except legacy fallback note in SKILL.md); tests/content/test_contracts.py passes

items.title: Create history/changes/ directory with README.md (FR-14)

narrative.Description: commands.md references history/changes/<name>/ but the directory doesn't exist. Create it with a README.md documenting the change lifecycle artifact structure. Closes #59.
narrative.Acceptance: history/changes/ directory exists; history/changes/README.md documents: what /deft:change creates, directory structure per change, lifecycle from proposal to archive; tests/content/test_structure.py passes

items.title: Refactor strategies/yolo.md to reference interview.md shared phases (FR-15)

narrative.Description: yolo.md duplicates ~80% of interview.md. Replace duplicated sections (sizing gate, chaining gate, acceptance gate, SPECIFICATION structure) with references to interview.md. Keep only yolo-specific content (Johnbot persona, auto-pick rules). Closes #23.
narrative.Acceptance: yolo.md references interview.md for: Sizing Gate, Chaining Gate, Acceptance Gate, SPECIFICATION structure; yolo.md is ≤60% of its current line count; Functional behavior unchanged (yolo still selects recommended options)

items.title: Add See also banner to strategies/speckit.md (FR-16)

narrative.Description: speckit.md is missing the standard **⚠️ See also** cross-reference banner at the top. Add it with links to interview.md and relevant strategy files. Closes #24.
narrative.Acceptance: speckit.md line 3-4 contains **⚠️ See also**: [...] banner; Banner links to at minimum: interview.md, discuss.md

items.title: Fix commands.md vBRIEF example vocabulary (FR-17)

narrative.Description: commands.md vBRIEF examples use status vocabulary that diverges from vbrief/vbrief.md. Update to use the canonical status enum. Closes #25.
narrative.Acceptance: commands.md vBRIEF file-level status examples use: draft | proposed | approved; commands.md vBRIEF task-level status examples use: pending | running | completed | blocked | cancelled; No use of 'todo', 'doing', 'done' in commands.md examples; no use of 'approved' as a task-level status

items.title: Clean core/project.md — remove Voxio Bot private content (FR-18)

narrative.Description: core/project.md contains private project config (Voxio Bot). Replace with a generic template showing example project config, or note it as a legacy location with a pointer to PROJECT.md.
narrative.Acceptance: core/project.md contains no reference to 'Voxio' or any private project; Content is either a generic template or a redirect to ./PROJECT.md; tests/content/test_standards.py Voxio xfail flips to passing

items.title: Create contracts/hierarchy.md — dual-hierarchy framework (FR-19)

narrative.Description: Document the two hierarchy lenses from #84/#89 debate: (1) durability axis (Standards > APIs > Specs > Code — what to invest in maintaining); (2) generative axis (Spec → Contracts → Code — what to write first). Explain when each applies. Closes #84 Phase 1 (hierarchy doc portion).
narrative.Acceptance: contracts/hierarchy.md exists with RFC2119 legend; Both axes documented with examples; File referenced from main.md or contracts/ README

items.title: Add adaptive teaching behavior to main.md (FR-20)

narrative.Description: Add to Agent Behavior section of main.md: ~ When a recommendation is accepted without question, be concise. ! When a recommendation is questioned or overridden, explain the reasoning. ⊗ Lecture unprompted on every decision. Closes #84 Phase 1 (adaptive teaching portion).
narrative.Acceptance: main.md Agent Behavior section contains the three adaptive teaching rules; Rules use RFC2119 symbols correctly

items.title: Add State WHY rule to strategies/interview.md (FR-21)

narrative.Description: Add ! rule to interview.md Interview Rules section: when making an opinionated recommendation, state the underlying principle in one sentence. Closes #84 Phase 1 (State WHY portion).
narrative.Acceptance: interview.md Interview Rules contains: ! When making an opinionated recommendation, state the principle (1 sentence); Rule positioned near existing RECOMMENDED marker rule

items.title: Write CONTRIBUTING.md with full contributor bootstrap (FR-22, NFR-5)

narrative.Description: Create CONTRIBUTING.md at repo root. Must cover: prerequisites (Go 1.22+, Python 3.11+, uv, task), dev environment setup, running tests (task test, task check), running CLI locally (python run or uv run python run), building the Go installer (go build ./cmd/deft-install/). Closes #67 AC item 3.
narrative.Acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md exists at repo root; Contains sections: Prerequisites, Dev Environment Setup, Running Tests, Running CLI Locally, Building the Installer; All commands listed are accurate and runnable; CONTRIBUTING.md documents `task check` as the authoritative pre-commit gate; explicitly states that a passing `task check` is the definition of ready-to-commit

items.title: Reframe README.md — remove Warping references (FR-23, depends on #89)

narrative.Description: Remove 'Warping Process', 'What is Warping?', 'Contributing to Warping' from README.md. Update tagline per #89 resolution. Cannot proceed until #89 decides the framework's positioning and tagline. Closes part of #84 Phase 2.
narrative.Acceptance: README.md contains no reference to 'Warping'; Tagline reflects #89 resolution; tests/content/test_standards.py Warping xfail flips to passing

items.title: Create meta/philosophy.md (FR-24, depends on #89)

narrative.Description: Create meta/philosophy.md with full contract hierarchy narrative per #84 Phase 2. Framing depends on #89 resolution (SDD vs. CDE vs. hybrid tagline).
narrative.Acceptance: meta/philosophy.md exists with RFC2119 legend; Covers: spec as primary IP, contracts as derived artifacts, code as renewable output; Consistent with #89 resolved framing

items.title: Write .github/workflows/ci.yml — lint + test on PRs and master pushes (FR-25, FR-26)

narrative.Description: GitHub Actions CI workflow triggering on pull_request and push to master. Jobs: (1) Python: ruff check, mypy tests/ (the shim run.py cannot be typed directly - exclude run and run.py from mypy per pyproject.toml, type-check the test suite instead), pytest tests/ with coverage. (2) Go: go test ./cmd/deft-install/ + go build ./cmd/deft-install/ for each platform matrix (linux/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64). main_test.go already exists so go test is zero-cost. Use current action versions. Closes #57.
narrative.Acceptance: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and is valid YAML; Python job runs: ruff, mypy tests/ (run and run.py excluded via pyproject.toml), pytest with coverage; Go job runs go test and builds installer for linux/amd64, darwin/arm64, and windows/amd64 (per NFR-3); Workflow triggers on pull_request and push to master; CI passes on a clean branch

items.title: Open GitHub issue for run CLI coverage tracking (NFR-2)

narrative.Description: Open a new GitHub issue titled 'Bring run CLI into test coverage measurement' in the Phase 4/5 backlog. Reference the exclusion in pyproject.toml. Include the rationale: run is terminal-only, excluded for now, refactor needed before coverage is meaningful.
narrative.Acceptance: GitHub issue exists with title containing 'run CLI' and 'coverage'; Issue is assigned to Phase 4 or Phase 5 milestone/label

items.title: Raise pyproject.toml coverage threshold to 85% and document run exclusion (NFR-1, NFR-2, FR-27)

narrative.Description: Update pyproject.toml: fail_under = 85. Add comment in [tool.coverage.run] omit section explaining why run and run.py are excluded (terminal-only CLI path; pending dedicated refactor issue). Resolves stated-vs-enforced coverage gap.
narrative.Acceptance: pyproject.toml fail_under = 85; omit entries for run and run.py include inline comment: '# terminal-only CLI; excluded pending #<issue>'; task test:coverage passes at >=85% threshold on the current test suite
